ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (admin/researcher) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the survey */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the survey */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the survey was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the survey was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "questions" (
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each question */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the survey */,
  "question_text" TEXT /* Text of the survey question */,
  "question_type" TEXT /* Type of question (multiple-choice, rating-scale, open-ended) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the question was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the question was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("question_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "question_options" (
  "option_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each option */,
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the question */,
  "option_text" TEXT /* Text of the question option */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the option was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the option was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("option_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the survey */,
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the question */,
  "response" TEXT /* Text of the response */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the participant */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the participant */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the participant */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the participant */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the participant */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the demographic entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the demographic entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_roles" (
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job role entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the participant */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the participant */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job role entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job role entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range of the participant */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the salary entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the salary entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "career_paths" (
  "career_path_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each career path entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "career_switch" TEXT /* Career switch information of the participant */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the career path entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the career path entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("career_path_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_satisfaction" (
  "job_satisfaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job satisfaction entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "salary_satisfaction" INTEGER /* Salary satisfaction rating of the participant */,
  "work_life_balance" INTEGER /* Work-life balance rating of the participant */,
  "coworkers" INTEGER /* Coworkers rating of the participant */,
  "management" INTEGER /* Management rating of the participant */,
  "upward_mobility" INTEGER /* Upward mobility rating of the participant */,
  "learning" INTEGER /* Learning rating of the participant */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job satisfaction entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job satisfaction entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_satisfaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "breaking_into_data_science" (
  "breaking_into_data_science_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response to breaking into data science question */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("breaking_into_data_science_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_importance" (
  "job_importance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response to job importance question */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_importance_id")
)
